title: [Feature] Hidden WSL2 install on Windows
body: ### Feature request description I am developing an app which I don't want users to have to know that containers/Podman/WSL2 is running. I want a more "hidden"/"quiet" install of Podman on windows. I am requesting that the first popup and the powershell popup on reboot be optionally removed. ### Suggest potential solution `podman machine init --hidden` If users don't have WSL2 installed, there will be a required reboot on `podman machine init`. Also, there are a couple of window prompts, I would like to be able to hid/silence the first window popup (perhaps with a `--hidden` flag for init command. The second window popup reboots Windows on confirmation, so that is necessary and can stay. There is also a Powershell pop-up on start-up that could be hidden if `--hidden` is used with init. ### Have you considered any alternatives? I'm not aware of any alternative method ### Additional context This will be useful for anyone embedding Podman into their application that doesn't want to annoy/burden users

comments.body: Fyi, I'm open to implementing this if this is something that would be accepted.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@jgresham that would be great if you have the bandwidth. We would definitely welcome the patch
@n1hility good to hear. I'll try to make some progress on this in the next several days

@jgresham something I just thought of, are you wanting to disable the WSL auto-installation from the podman command? Or did you just want to mute graphical prompts? Did you want your app to be in control of the install process?

@n1hility I just wanted to mute the graphical prompts as much as possible (leave reboot prompt, but disable first prompt and terminal that comes up after reboot). It would still leave Podman control over installing WSL.
